name: Apo Reef
conservationStatus: Apo Reef Natural Park, managed by the Apo Reef Protected Area Management Board. Philippines' largest contiguous reef system.
description:
Apo Reef is the largest contiguous coral reef system in the Philippines and the second-largest in the world after Australia's Great Barrier Reef. The atoll-like structure covers 34 km² of reef area in the Mindoro Strait.

The reef's remote location in the Sulu Sea has helped preserve its ecological integrity, though enforcement of no-take zones remains challenging. Apo Reef supports exceptional coral diversity with nearly 400 species, and is an important aggregation site for sharks, mantas, and marine turtles. The reef is a critical component of the broader Coral Triangle ecosystem.
